topic: LangGraph Tutorial 2026: Build Stateful AI Agents with Graphs

competitors.title: How to Build an AI Agent with LangGraph Python in 14 Steps [2026]
key_points: Comprehensive 14-step tutorial for building production-grade AI agents with LangGraph
Covers installation, state schema, tools, graph nodes, conditional edges, memory checkpointing
Includes human-in-the-loop approval, multi-agent collaboration, integration testing
Details deployment with LangGraph Platform and monitoring with LangSmith

Contains troubleshooting guide for 10 common LangGraph issues
Comparison with other frameworks (CrewAI vs AutoGen vs LangGraph)

competitors.title: LangGraph Guide 2026 — Stateful AI Agents & Graph-Based LLM Orchestration
key_points: Core LangGraph concepts: state, nodes, edges, conditional edges, checkpointers
LangGraph vs LangChain comparison table highlighting key differences

Open-source with 60k+ GitHub stars, Python and JavaScript implementations

stats.fact: LangGraph version 1.1.6 released April 10, 2026 with over 126,000 GitHub stars
LangGraph has 60,000+ GitHub stars (Singularity Moments) and supports both Python and JavaScript
Apache 2.0 open-source license, first released in 2023
Typical agent query with 2-3 tool calls using GPT-4o costs $0.02-0.05 in API calls
Complete tutorial project costs under $2 in total API costs for all steps
LangGraph Platform (formerly LangGraph Cloud) provides managed deployment with REST API
Works with local models via Ollama, requiring at least 16GB RAM and GPU for acceptable inference speed
Native checkpointing supports SQLite, PostgreSQL, and in-memory persistence
